jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Experience in sales or business development in the software/technology industry
- Experience in sales or account management
jobs.description:
Would you like to be part of the AWS Sales team responsible for increasing the adoption of our services by engaging Independent Software Vendor (ISVs)? Do you have the business acumen and technical background to establish AWS as a key technology platform provider and partner?

As an ISV Account Manager, you will have the exciting opportunity to help grow ISVs and shape their future. You will be responsible for a select number of engaged accounts that generate significant revenue, typically have a complex organizational structure and operate in multiple geographies.

AWS Sales, Marketing, and Global Services (SMGS) is responsible for driving revenue, adoption, and growth from the largest and fastest growing small- and mid-market accounts to enterprise-level customers including public sector.

This role is based in Lux.

Key job responsibilities
• Increase the market penetration of AWS into the ISV engaged accounts in Benelux and support their business growth.
• Teaming with the customer’s board, IT, digital and cloud leaders to build strategic relationships.
• Articulating a clear vision and generating enthusiasm, while impacting all business groups.
• Responsible for working at the most strategic level within the customer and implementing a broad strategy for earning customer adoption, acceptance and service implementation.
• Working with all appropriate AWS resources to support customer interests, accelerate ISV SaaS modernizations and migrations, deliver revenues growth.
• Build credibility with, earns the trust of, and influences C-level client contacts and internal stakeholders.
• Set the standard for quality and impact of written documentation at AWS.
• Drive revenue and market share in a defined territory.
• Meet or exceed quarterly revenue targets. Develop and execute against a comprehensive account/territory plan.
• Maintain a robust sales pipeline
• Work with partners to extend reach & drive adoption
• Develop long-term strategic relationships with key accounts
• Manage forecast and business accuracy.

A day in the life
Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

Mentorship and Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

About the team
Inclusive Team Culture
Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

jobs.preferred_qualifications:
- AWS certification, such as, AWS Solutions Architect, or a similar cloud certification, or Cloud Technology Certification

We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. jobs.title: Account Manager - ISVs, AGS-EMEA-Field-ISV Sales-North

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree or equivalent
- 2+ years of equivalent experience
- Experience working with machine learning data workflows or similar technical domains
- Hands-on experience in leadership and mentoring
- Strategic thinking about workflow optimization and process improvement
- Effective stakeholder communication skills with the ability to influence decision-makers and customers
- Domain expertise in STEM, language, or technical domains highly valued.
jobs.description:
The Senior Quality Auditor serves as a specialist who becomes the organization's deepest expert on 1-2 complex machine learning data workflows while maintaining hands-on audit execution. This role within the Machine Learning Data Operations job family leads Quality Audit Design (QuAD) teams for high-complexity workflows, handles the most ambiguous audit scenarios, and generates workflow-specific quality analysis that drives customer insights and workflow optimization.

Key job responsibilities
Workflow Expertise Development

- Become the organization's deepest expert on 1-2 assigned complex workflows through intensive pilot participation and continuous learning
- Participate in workflow pilots as an annotator to build foundational knowledge before production begins
- Develop comprehensive understanding of workflow conventions, edge cases, and quality requirements
Team Leadership
- Lead QuAD teams of 3-5 Quality Auditors for Template 2 (High-Engagement Support) workflows
- Provide day-to-day leadership and make real-time quality decisions during iteration phases
- Handle escalations from team members and serve as the go-to expert for workflow-specific questions
- Mentor and develop Quality Auditor team members through coaching, feedback, and knowledge sharing
Audit Execution
- Execute audits on the most ambiguous and complex scenarios, reducing escalations to Quality Specialists
- Maintain 60-70% hands-on audit execution to preserve expertise required for real-time decision-making
- Validate team audit decisions and ensure consistency across QuAD team members
- Support Template 1 workflows by auditing high-complexity scenarios requiring specialized judgment
Analysis & Insights
- Generate workflow-specific deep dives and thematic analysis providing insights beyond conventions adherence scores
- Identify error patterns, convention evolution, and areas of ambiguity within assigned workflows
- Provide actionable recommendations for workflow optimization and quality improvement
- Partner with DSLs and customers on workflow-specific quality insights
Strategic Collaboration
- Provide input to audit strategy design led by Quality Specialist I
- Collaborate with Quality Specialists on cross-workflow patterns and tactical improvements
- Participate in workflow design discussions with customers and stakeholders
- Support new-tool onboarding for operationalized and bootstrapped interfaces
- Track record of generating actionable insights from quality data.
jobs.description_short: The Senior Quality Auditor serves as a specialist who becomes the organization's deepest expert on 1-2 complex machine learning data workflows while maintaining hands-on audit execution. This role within
jobs.preferred_qualifications:
- Experience with explainable machine learning and artificial intelligence methodologies and tools
- Experience in leadership roles, overseeing teams conducting data analysis, process improvements, creative problem solving, and cross-functional collaboration
- Experience using data and metrics to drive actionable insights at scale
- Experience that includes strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and effective communication abilities
- Experience handling administrative details independently, while exercising good judgment in keeping team members adequately informed
- Familiarity with audit methodologies and quality frameworks
- Experience participating in pilot phases and workflow development

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. jobs.title: Senior Quality Auditor, Artificial General Intelligence

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree or equivalent, or 2+ years of Amazon experience
- 7+ years’ direct management experience, including a salaried, hourly and agency workforce, in a logistics, fulfillment, last-mile manufacturing, engineering, production or distribution environment
- Demonstrated track record of delivering performance and financial based results in high pressure environments
- Experience with performance metrics and process improvement and lean techniques
- Experience with the full lifecycle staffing, including interviewing, hiring, performance management, promotions and terminations
- Strong analytical capability; track record of digging into data and finding solutions for a variety of operational problems
- Excellent communication skills (including local language and English, written and verbal)
- Experience managing third party resources on a regional/national scale
- Experience managing contingent workforce in a business with peak seasons
- Candidates must be flexible to work a variety of hours as business demands, including overnight, weekends and holidays
jobs.description:
AMZL Sr. Station Leaders are responsible for all budgetary, people development and operations objectives for an Amazon Logistics Delivery Station (DS). Additional responsibilities include managing and leading a team of Operations, Area and Shift Managers, coaching and mentoring the team to ensure performance objectives are met, building positive employee relations and building leadership bench strength within the DS.

Key job responsibilities
- Responsible for the overall safety, quality, performance, customer experience and people development for all delivery station operations
- Drives vision for station team and creation of quality initiatives, process change initiatives and other lean initiatives to enable their functional area and ultimately the DS to meet and exceed business plan
- Problem-solves for closing gaps between performance and goals
- Will set and clarify requirements and expectations for Operations, Area and Shift Managers. Measures performance, provides feedback, and holds Ops Managers accountable for their performance and the performance of their departments
- Manages an environment concerning the delivery of Amazon orders direct to our customers, subject to ever rising volume and complexity, operating on a 24/7 basis
- Manages day-to-day relationships with multiple Delivery Service Providers (DSPs)
- Presents operational metrics regularly to the senior leadership team
- Hires and develops top talent. Creates and implements a talent plan for the site including talent acquisition, performance and career management, learning and development, succession planning, and employee engagement
- Leverages the Operations, Area and Shift Managers by sourcing and nurturing ideas, and rolling them into the creation of improvement plans for the functional area
- Cultivates strong culture and engagement throughout all levels of the delivery station team
- Takes proactive steps to ensure that best practices are shared across all departments, shifts and among the network.
- Works with key business partners such as Transport & Sortation, DSP Management, Finance, Loss Prevention, IT, HR, ACES and Engineering to build and secure support and resources for projects and initiatives in their area, as well as providing needed support and resources for other staff initiatives

- Works with key business partners such as Transport & Sortation, DSP Management, Finance, Loss Prevention, IT, HR, ACES and Engineering to build and secure support and resources for projects and initiatives in their area, as well as providing needed support and resources for other staff initiatives

jobs.title: Senior Station Manager

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of technical product or program management experience
- 7+ years of working directly with engineering teams experience
- 3+ years of software development experience
- 5+ years of technical program management working directly with software engineering teams experience
- Experience managing programs across cross functional teams, building processes and coordinating release schedules
jobs.description:
Amazon Leo is an initiative to launch a constellation of Low Earth Orbit satellites that will provide low-latency, high-speed broadband connectivity to unserved and underserved communities around the world.

As a Sr. Technical Program Manager you will own driving the execution, implementation, development, and test of Amazon Leo SW and tooling built to support it. The role will interface with the cross-functional engineering and program management teams to build the software and test infrastructure we own. Because of the scale of the mission, you will be focused on driving automation and operational excellence — identifying areas of improvement and initiating changes to improve the productivity of the fleet.

Export Control Requirement:
Due to applicable export control laws and regulations, candidates must be a U.S. citizen or national, U.S. permanent resident (i.e., current Green Card holder), or lawfully admitted into the U.S. as a refugee or granted asylum.

Required Qualifications
- 5+ years of technical program management or related experience
- Strong technical background with ability to understand complex software systems
- Deep understanding of SDLC and release management practices
- Proven track record managing programs across multiple teams and dependencies
- Experience with distributed systems or large-scale deployments
- Excellent organizational, planning, and execution skills
- Strong communication and stakeholder management abilities
- Experience with Agile, Scrum, or similar development methodologies

Technical Knowledge
- Software development lifecycle and release processes
- CI/CD pipelines and DevOps practices
- System architecture and integration concepts
- Testing strategies (unit, integration, system, regression)
- Version control and branching strategies
- Deployment methodologies and rollback procedures
- Infrastructure and cloud platforms
- Risk management and quality assurance principles

Program Management Skills
- Release planning and scheduling
- Dependency mapping and critical path analysis
- Risk identification and mitigation
- Cross-functional coordination and influence
- Metrics definition and tracking
- Process design and optimization
- Stakeholder management and communication
- Problem-solving and decision-making under pressure

You Will Be Successful If You
- Partner effectively with SDM to balance technical excellence with program delivery
- Think about scalability and design processes for hundreds/thousands of satellite deployments
- Navigate ambiguity and drive clarity in complex, multi-team environments
- Balance competing priorities and make data-driven trade-off decisions
- Proactively identify risks before they become issues
- Communicate complex technical concepts to non-technical audiences
- Drive accountability without direct authority
- Adapt quickly to changing requirements and operational needs
- Foster collaboration and build strong relationships across teams
